div.p: This diagram specifies the logic for transforming JIT-compiled stack frames back into interpreter-compatible operand stacks during a deoptimization event.

ul.li: Memory Direction: Offset 0x00 represents the base of the operand stack.
Ordering: Descending sort ensures that we never write to an address that hasn't been properly calculated, preventing stack smashing during in-place transitions.
Alignment: The resulting vSP must be aligned to 16 bytes for System V AMD64 ABI compatibility if the interpreter calls external C functions.
Context: RegisterDump populated via signal handler or trampoline.

ol.li: Stack pointer correctly points to top of Slot 2.
Control flow ready to resume at Interpreter Loop.

g.text: Offset | ID | Target Slot | Source Reg/Disp | Size
